rulings.Case Title: RAPHAEL MERRIMAN ET AL VS. HING WAH CONSTRUCTION, INC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT HING WAH CONSTRUCTION, INC, TONY LEE DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Plaintiffs First and Third Causes of Action are overruled because Plaintiffs sufficiently pled a cause of action that is not time barred. Plaintiffs Fourth and Fifth Causes of Action are sustained with leave to amend because Plaintiffs because there is no alleged timeframe in which the causes of action accrued. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Order Quashing Subpoena Duces Tecum As To Mei Ling Fang And Or Shuzhen Tu
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT DEMAS YAN Motion For Order Quashing Subpoena Duces Tecum As To Mei Ling Fang And Or Shuzhen Tu is DENIED. Plaintiff is awarded sanctions in the amount of $3,450. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TON KIANG RESTAURANT, INC. VS. WILLIAM WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT TOM KIANG RESTAURANT, INC. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED as to the Third Cause of Action, Unjust Enrichment, due to the improper inclusion of allegations that a valid contract exists. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment And For Entry Of Interlocutory Judgment And Accounting Related To Partition
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019 line 4. PLAINTIFF REEF SHAFER MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment And For Entry Of Interlocutory Judgment And Accounting Related To Partition is DENIED under California Code of Civil Procedure section 437 subdivision (c) paragraph (c). Plaintiffs demonstrate that there is a triable issue of material fact regarding waiver remaining, as evidenced by the declaration of Suzanne Romaine, and Exhibit A of Tara Macomber's Declaration. 2. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections. GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART. 3. Request for judicial notice is GRANTED under California Evidence Code §452(d). =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CHI CHI BEIGNET INC DOING BUSINESS AS BISCUITS VS. SAEED KHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts Order Of September 3, 2019 Denying Injunctive Relief Pursuant To Ccp 1008
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019 line 5. PLAINTIFF CHI CHI BEIGNET INC DOING BUSINESS AS BISCUITS Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration Of The Courts Order Of September 3, 2019 Denying Injunctive Relief Pursuant To Ccp 1008 is CONTINUED to January 29, 2020. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA A. CARVAJAL VS. SF MISSION PROPERTIES, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019 line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT MARIA CARVAJAL DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Cross-Complainant failed to sufficiently allege that there is an actual controversy as a predicate to obtaining declaratory relief. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JULIANA CLEGG VS. WILMINGTON TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 24, 2019 line 7. DEFENDANT WILMINGTON TRUST, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT 1. Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Defendant Wilmington Trust, National Association, Not In Its Individual Capacity, But Solely as Trustee for MFRA Trust 2014-2 (erroneously sued herein as Wilmington Trust, National Association)'s Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend on the condition that Plaintiff is able to assert, in good faith, facts that support each cause of action. Plaintiff fails to sufficiently allege facts to support any of the four causes of action. 2. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice. Defendant Wilmington Trust, National Association, Not In Its Individual Capacity, But Solely as Trustee for MFRA Trust 2014-2 (erroneously sued herein as Wilmington Trust, National Association)'s Request for Judicial Notice is GRANTED as follows: 1. Deed of Trust recorded July 31, 2007 {Granted under California Evidence code § 452(h)} 2. Assignment of Deed of Trust recorded November 15, 2017 {Granted under California Evidence code § 452(h)} 3. Preliminary Public Report for the subject property {Granted under California Evidence code § 452(c)} 4. Conditional Subdivision Public Report {Granted under California Evidence code § 452(c)} 5. Amended Conditional Subdivision Public Report {Granted under California Evidence code § 452(c)} 6. Complaint for Declaratory Relief in CGC-17-558200 {Granted under California Evidence code § 452(d)} 7. First Amended Complaint in CGC-17-558200 {Granted under California Evidence code § 452(d)} 8. Request for Dismissal in CGC-17-558200 {Granted under California Evidence code § 452(d)} 9. Notice of Intention for a Limited Term Final Public Report {Grant in part, only to the extent that is a notice of the intent, not to the substance of the notice under California Evidence code § 452(h)} 10. Department of Real Estate Limited Term Final Subdivision Public Report {Grant under California Evidence code § 452(c)} 11. Notice of Default {Grant under California Evidence code § 452(h)} 12. Notice of Trustee's Sale {Grant under California Evidence code § 452(h)} 13. Notice of Pendency of Action (Lis Pendens) {Grant under California Evidence code § 452(d)} =(501/REQ)
